description: Back at the Playground, Mack apologizes to Coulson and May for his earlier outburst, expressing regret and a desire to make amends. Simmons brings up the possibility that Raina is a contagion and suggests lethal force may be necessary to prevent further catastrophe. May and Coulson weigh these moral and practical dilemmas amid ongoing uncertainty.

transcript.line: Thanks for the schematics, sir. Ventilation system's all good. And, sir... I want to apologize for my outburst in there. It was uncalled for. I have a little trouble dealing with my anger sometimes.
Yeah, something I'm working on, but this alien stuff messed with my head, and, uh... and losing Trip brought back a lot of hard memories.
I hope I-I didn't blow my chance to work on Lola one day.
After that little R.C. version you built for me, I'm genuinely considering it, although I couldn't figure out how to make it fly.
Sir, I do think Mack brought up a matter we should consider.
I'm aware the team hunting Raina has orders to capture, not kill.
But this might be some sort of contagion or even a plague inadvertently set loose. Plagues must be understood, yes... but eventually eradicated.
Are you saying that you want to put Raina down, Agent Simmons?
No, of course not, uh... I'm just saying that if they have to, it may not be the worst thing.
